title: Evaluation of constant value failed
explanation: This error occurs when the compiler tries to evaluate a constant expression at compile time but encounters an error, such as integer overflow, division by zero, or an out-of-bounds array access.
example_bad:
const X: u8 = 255 + 1; // Error: overflow
const Y: i32 = 10 / 0; // Error: division by zero
const ARR: [i32; 3] = [1, 2, 3];
const Z: i32 = ARR[5]; // Error: index out of bounds
error_message: error[E0080]: evaluation of constant value failed — attempt to compute `255_u8 + 1_u8`, which would overflow

fixes.code:
const X: u16 = 255 + 1; // Use a larger type
const Y: i32 = 10 / 2; // Fix division

const X: u8 = 255u8.wrapping_add(1); // Wraps to 0
const Y: u8 = 255u8.saturating_add(1); // Stays at 255

tip: Constant evaluation happens at compile time. The compiler catches errors that would be runtime panics in non-const code. Use checked arithmetic methods for intentional wrapping.
